description: Connect to SharePoint Online to browse sites, document libraries, and pull documents into your knowledge base via Microsoft Graph API.

operations.name: Health Check
operations.description: Verify the connection — checks the access token and that SharePoint is reachable. Returns the signed-in user and a clear error message if anything is misconfigured.

operations.name: List Sites
operations.description: List the tenant root site plus any sites in the SharePoint search index. Newly-created sites have a 5-15 min indexing delay.

parameters.name: query
parameters.label: Search Query
parameters.description: Keyword to search for sites (use * for all)

operations.name: Lookup Site by URL
operations.description: Find a SharePoint site directly by its URL (bypasses the search index — works for sites that haven't been indexed yet)

parameters.name: url
parameters.label: SharePoint Site URL
parameters.description: Full URL e.g. https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MarketingTeam

operations.name: List Document Libraries
operations.description: List all document libraries (drives) in a SharePoint site

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.label: Site ID
parameters.description: The SharePoint site ID

operations.name: List Files & Folders
operations.description: List files and folders in a document library or subfolder

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.label: Drive ID
parameters.description: The document library (drive) ID

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Folder ID
parameters.description: Folder ID to browse into (leave empty for root)

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.label: Item Type Filter
parameters.description: Restrict results to a single item type. Use 'Folders only' to iterate over subfolders, or 'Files only' to iterate over files. Useful when a folder contains a mix of both and the workflow only cares about one kind.

parameters.name: top
parameters.label: Max Results
parameters.description: Maximum items to return (max 999)

operations.name: Get File Info
operations.description: Get metadata for a specific file or folder including size, modified date, and download URL

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.label: Drive ID
parameters.description: The document library (drive) ID

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: The file or folder ID

operations.name: Download File
operations.description: Download a file from SharePoint, preserving the original filename

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.label: Drive ID
parameters.description: The document library (drive) ID

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: The file ID to download

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path OR a subfolder under /uploads. Leave blank to use /uploads root.

operations.name: Search Files
operations.description: Search for files across a document library by name or content

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.label: Drive ID
parameters.description: The document library (drive) ID

parameters.name: query
parameters.label: Search Query
parameters.description: Text to search for in file names and content

operations.name: List Folder (by Path)
operations.description: List items in a folder by path — workflow-friendly alternative to list_items. Supports filtering by name pattern and modified date.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Folder Path
parameters.description: Relative to drive root, e.g. 'Shared Documents/Inbox'

parameters.name: file_pattern
parameters.label: File Pattern
parameters.description: Optional glob filter (e.g. '*.pdf')

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: ISO date/datetime — only return files modified after this (for 'new files' workflows)

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.label: Item Type Filter
parameters.description: Restrict results to a single item type. Applied after file_pattern.

operations.name: Download File (by Path)
operations.description: Download a single file by its path. Preserves the original filename.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Relative to drive root, e.g. 'Reports/Q3.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path OR a subfolder under /uploads

operations.name: Download All Files in Folder
operations.description: Batch-download all files (matching optional name pattern and/or modified date) from a folder. Files keep their original names.

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: ISO date/datetime (for 'new files since last run')

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path (e.g. 'C:\temp\downloads') OR a subfolder under /uploads (e.g. 'reports/2026'). Created automatically.

operations.name: Upload File to Folder
operations.description: Upload a local file to a SharePoint folder. Requires the Files.ReadWrite.All scope (reconnect after the integration update to pick up the new scope). Handles large files via chunked upload sessions. Specify the destination using EITHER Parent Folder ID (recommended for workflows iterating by id) OR Destination Folder Path. If both are provided, Parent Folder ID wins.

parameters.name: parent_item_id
parameters.label: Parent Folder ID
parameters.description: SharePoint item_id of the destination folder. Useful when the destination comes from a previous list operation — pass a variable holding the folder's id.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder Path
parameters.description: Drive-relative folder path, e.g. 'Shared Documents/Reports'. Required only if Parent Folder ID is blank.

parameters.name: source_file_path
parameters.label: Local File Path
parameters.description: Local server path. From workflow: pass a variable like '${generated_report}'

operations.name: Upload Content as File
operations.description: Upload string or JSON content as a file. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

operations.name: Delete File
operations.description: Delete a file by item_id or by file_path. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

operations.name: Create Folder
operations.description: Create a folder (and any missing parent folders) in a document library. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Folder Path
parameters.description: Path to create, e.g. 'CustomerA/Processed/2026-Q2'. Parent folders are created automatically.

operations.name: Move File
operations.description: Move a file to a different folder within the same drive. Optionally rename during the move. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id, e.g. 'Inbox/report.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Target folder path, e.g. 'Processed/2026-Q2'

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Filename
parameters.description: Optionally rename the file during the move

operations.name: Copy File
operations.description: Copy a file to a different folder within the same drive. The original file is kept in place. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id, e.g. 'Inbox/report.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Target folder path, e.g. 'Archive/2026'

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Filename
parameters.description: Optionally rename the copy

operations.name: Rename File
operations.description: Rename a file or folder in place. Requires Files.ReadWrite.All.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Name
parameters.description: New filename including extension, e.g. 'PROCESSED_report.pdf'

operations.name: Import to Knowledge Base
operations.description: Download a SharePoint file and add it to an agent's knowledge base for AI-powered retrieval

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.label: Drive ID
parameters.description: The document library (drive) ID

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: The file ID to import

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.label: Agent ID
parameters.description: The agent to add this document to as knowledge

parameters.name: description
parameters.label: Description
parameters.description: Optional description for this knowledge item

operations.name: Bulk Import Folder to Knowledge Base
operations.description: Bulk version of Import to Knowledge — queues every matching file from a folder for background import. Filter by name pattern and/or modified date.

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: ISO date/datetime — only import files modified after this

setup_instructions:
## SharePoint Online Setup

### Step 1: Register Azure App
1. Go to [Azure Portal → App Registrations](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade)
2. Click **New registration**
3. Name: `AI Hub SharePoint Integration`
4. Supported account types: **Accounts in this organizational directory only** (single tenant) or **Accounts in any organizational directory** (multi-tenant)
5. Redirect URI: **Web** → `{callback_url}`
6. Click **Register**

### Step 2: Configure API Permissions
1. Go to **API permissions** → **Add a permission**
2. Select **Microsoft Graph** → **Delegated permissions**
3. Add: `Sites.Read.All`, `Files.Read.All`, `offline_access`
4. Click **Grant admin consent** (requires admin)

### Step 3: Create Client Secret
1. Go to **Certificates & secrets** → **New client secret**
2. Set an expiry and click **Add**
3. Copy the secret **Value** immediately

### Step 4: Configure in AI Hub
1. Copy the **Application (client) ID** from the Overview page
2. Store in Local Secrets:
   - `OAUTH_SHAREPOINT_ONLINE_CLIENT_ID` → Application (client) ID
   - `OAUTH_SHAREPOINT_ONLINE_CLIENT_SECRET` → Client secret value
3. Click **Connect** and sign in with your Microsoft account

### Note on Permissions
An Azure AD admin may need to grant tenant-wide consent for `Sites.Read.All` before users can connect.
